name: Chainlink

events.notes: Q2 2026 quarterly unlock; ~14.9M to Binance, ~4.1M to staking rewards multi-sig
Estimated Q3 2026 unlock based on historical 10-20M quarterly pattern
Estimated Q4 2026 unlock based on historical 10-20M quarterly pattern

unlocks.summary: Chainlink operates a predictable quarterly token release schedule of 10-20M LINK (~1.4-2.7% per quarter). As of April 2026, ~272.9M LINK remain unlocked from the 1B max supply, representing a gradual supply increase as the protocol transitions from vesting to emission-based rewards. No major cliff releases remain; unlock pressure is moderate and evenly distributed across 2026.
unlocks.caveats: Exact H2 2026 unlock amounts estimated based on historical patterns; official schedule beyond Q2 2026 not found in free sources. The transition from vesting to staking emissions complicates lock classification.

mechanism.value_accrual_summary: Stakers capture value via emission-based rewards (4.32% APY) with future transition to user fee-based staking rewards. Chainlink Reserve captures protocol fees and converts them to LINK for staking rewards.

inflation.rationale: Current annualized inflation of ~11% driven by staking emissions and quarterly token releases (~70M/yr). Trend is declining as vesting phases out and the protocol transitions from emission-based to fee-based staking rewards, reducing need for new token creation.

data_collection_notes: Data collected April 2026 via WebSearch and public tokenomics aggregators. Chainlink official blog and staking portal (chain.link, blog.chain.link) blocked by network proxy; sourced equivalent data from CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, Everstake, tokenomist.ai, and news archives. Unlock schedule inferred from quarterly patterns and recent April 2026 release; H2 2026 amounts estimated. Staking APY reflects v0.2 base floor rate (4.32% after delegation rewards). Inflation rate calculated from observed YoY supply growth; future rate depends on fee implementation timeline and emission phase-out. Key uncertainty: exact timing and magnitude of fee-based value accrual to stakers.
